title: Automation Safety
description: Understand the safety systems that protect against automation loops and issues

sections.title: Safety Systems Overview
sections.content:
MUDPuppy includes several safety systems to protect you from automation issues:

- **Recursion Limits** - Prevents infinite alias expansion
- **Command Queue** - Limits commands sent per tick
- **Trigger Cooldown** - Prevents trigger spam
- **Circuit Breaker** - Detects and pauses problematic automation

These systems keep your client stable and prevent accidental damage.

sections.title: Recursion Limits
sections.content:
Aliases can trigger other aliases (alias A calls alias B). The recursion limit prevents infinite loops:

**Maximum depth: 3 levels**

Example:
1. You type 'a'
2. Alias A expands to 'b'
3. Alias B expands to 'c'
4. Alias C would expand but hits the limit

This prevents situations where aliases call each other endlessly.

sections.title: Command Queue Limits
sections.content:
The command queue prevents flooding the server with too many commands:

**Maximum: 200 commands per dispatch**

If your automation generates more than 200 commands, the extras are queued for the next tick.

This protects your connection and prevents triggering rate limits on the MUD server.

sections.title: Trigger Cooldown
sections.content:
Each trigger has a cooldown period (in milliseconds):

- After a trigger fires, it waits before firing again
- Default cooldown: 5000ms (5 seconds)
- Set per-trigger in the Trigger Editor

Cooldowns prevent triggers from executing too frequently, especially when game messages repeat.

sections.title: Circuit Breaker
sections.content:
The circuit breaker detects problematic automation patterns and pauses automation:

**What triggers the circuit breaker:**
- Rapid command generation (potential infinite loop)
- Too many commands in a short time
- Suspicious automation patterns

**When triggered:**
- Automation is paused automatically
- You see a warning banner
- You can Resume or Disable automation

sections.title: Resuming Automation
sections.content:
When automation is paused by the circuit breaker:

**Option 1: Resume Automation**
- Clears the circuit breaker
- Re-enables all triggers and aliases
- Use this if the pause was a false positive

**Option 2: Disable Automation**
- Turns off all automation for this connection
- Remains disabled until you manually re-enable
- Use this if there's a real problem with your automation

sections.title: Why Automation May Pause
sections.content:
Common reasons automation pauses:

1. **Infinite Alias Loop** - Two aliases trigger each other
2. **Trigger Spamming** - A trigger matches constantly repeated text
3. **Too Many Commands** - Automation generates commands faster than the queue can process
4. **Pattern Too Broad** - A trigger matches almost every message

**To prevent pauses:**
- Use specific trigger patterns
- Set appropriate cooldowns
- Test automation in a safe area first
- Use variables instead of hardcoded values
